header.title: Auto Clipper
header.subtitle: Turn long videos into viral shorts instantly.

main.subtitle_label: Embed Subtitles into video (can be changed per-clip)
main.burn_subtitles_desc: Embed captions directly into the video
main.project_title_desc: Used to name the output folder neatly.

settings.title: Settings
settings.api_key_note: Encrypted and stored securely on your device
settings.api_key_req: API key required for AI mode

faq.title: How to Use
faq.desc: Easily turn long videos into vertical viral clips:
faq.step1_desc: Supports standard links, youtu.be, and Shorts.
faq.step2_desc: Open Settings (⚙️) and enter your AI API Key.
faq.step3_desc: Click generate and let AI find the best highlights automatically!
faq.step4_desc: Click Download MP4 to save the final video.
faq.note_desc: Ensure your API key has active quota/credits.

history.title: 🕒 History
history.delete_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete this history? All related clip files and metadata will be permanently removed from local storage.
history.ai_correct_desc: Enter a custom instruction to re-run the AI clip selection from scratch.
history.ai_prompt_placeholder: e.g. Focus only on funny coding moments

toast.api_key_req: API Key is required! Please configure it in Settings.

busy.warning: Please wait until the process completes. The app is temporarily locked so no other processes run concurrently — do not close the app.

faqs.a: Yes, the app itself is free and open-source. However, you will need an API Key from OpenAI or Gemini which may incur usage charges on their respective platforms.

faqs.q: What types of videos are supported?
faqs.a: Currently, we support any public YouTube videos of any duration, including YouTube Shorts.

faqs.q: Why does downloading a YouTube video fail?
faqs.a: Some YouTube videos are DRM-protected, copyrighted, or require a login. Please ensure the video is public.

faqs.q: Can I change the subtitle font?
faqs.a: Currently, font options are limited, but you can change the subtitle style (Standard / Karaoke) during the re-render process.
It heavily depends on the original video's length and your PC's performance, as the actual video rendering (ffmpeg) runs locally on your machine.
